title: Adenocalymma pachypus
taxonomy: Adenocalymma pachypus is classified within the family Bignoniaceae, commonly known as the trumpet vine or catalpa family. This family is part of the order Lamiales, which includes various flowering plant species. The order Lamiales is further classified under the larger clade Asterids, a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Solanaceae and Scrophulariaceae. Adenocalymma pachypus, as a species, is often placed within the genus Adenocalymma, which comprises around 50 species of tropical plants. The classification of Adenocalymma pachypus reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.

intro: Adenocalymma pachypus is a plant species of the family Bignoniaceae, commonly known as a tropical vine with medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of various gastrointestinal disorders, such as dyspepsia and irritable bowel syndrome, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Adenocalymma pachypus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Adenocalymma pachypus include teas and extracts, as well as standardized extracts in capsule or tablet form, which are used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms associated with gastrointestinal issues.
